prose:
Fukui electrophilicity index (f+)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’), PySCF RKS with exact four-center integrals (no density fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied pyridine.xyz (no re-optimization; single-point calculations on the fixed geometry for N, N±1 charge states; cation/anion doublets generated automatically). No solvent (explicitly gas phase).

Calculation used chemkit skill `fukui-reactivity` (engine subcommand `fukui`). Live SCF output was written during the run to a timestamped `.out` file; final structured results stored in `pyridine_fukui_dft.json` (see `cli_invocation` in JSON for full command line).

Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states. The integrity checker reports:
• "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "common header present"
All checks passed; integrity.trustworthy = true.

Result (headline): The most electrophilic site is atom 1 (N) with f⁺ = 0.48422572 (dimensionless).

No experimental comparison was requested or provided.
